# botyard_tool (Data Source)

Looks up a single tool from the organization's tool catalog by its composite slug, exposing its `id` for use with `botyard_bot_tool_assignment`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Resolve a single tool's id from its composite slug, then assign it to a bot.
data "botyard_tool" "list_repos" {
  slug = "mcp:botyard:github_list_repos"
}

resource "botyard_bot_tool_assignment" "example" {
  bot_slug = botyard_bot.example.slug
  tool_ids = [data.botyard_tool.list_repos.id]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `slug` (String) Composite slug of the tool to look up (e.g. `mcp:botyard:github_list_repos`).

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp (RFC 3339).
- `description` (String) What the tool does. May be null.
- `domain` (String) Tool grouping (e.g. `github`, `conversations`).
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the tool is globally enabled.
- `id` (String) Unique tool identifier (UUID). Use this as `tool_ids` in `botyard_bot_tool_assignment`.
- `mcp_server` (String) Name of the MCP server backing the tool (null for non-MCP tools).
- `name` (String) Human-readable display name.
- `org_id` (String) Owning organization ID (null for platform tools).
- `runtime` (String) Where the tool executes — `mcp` or `openclaw`.
- `runtime_tool_name` (String) Internal runtime name (MCP function name or OpenClaw tool ID).
- `updated_at` (String) Last-update timestamp (RFC 3339).
